title: quercus dumosa var. populifolia
intro: Quercus dumosa var. populifolia, also known as the Himalayan oak, is a variety of deciduous tree native to the Himalayan region. The leaves, bark, and fruits of this plant have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Quercus dumosa var. populifolia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and act as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark can be made into teas, decoctions, or infusions, while the fruits can be used to make jams and other sweet preparations.
